articles.title: I Am Artemis: Peter Rossoni
articles.description: Listen to this audio excerpt from Peter Rossoni, Orion Artemis II Optical Communications System flight manager: As a child, Peter Rossoni watched the Apollo missions launch with his family. In April 2026, he became a part of NASA’s Artemis II mission, helping enable communications as astronauts jour

articles.title: 'Guess I'll be waiting a minute to surf again': Artemis 2 moon astronaut Christina Koch shares her recovery journey
articles.description: A week after the Artemis 2 moon mission ended, NASA astronaut Christina Koch struggled to walk in a straight line during a blindfold test. But it's all part of readjusting to life on Earth.

articles.title: The governance gap: Why orbital data centers need certification before they scale
articles.description: For more than two decades in sourcing and supply-chain architecture, we’ve watched industries scale only when their supply chains become predictable, certifiable and repeatable. Orbital and lunar data centers are now approaching that same inflection point. Hardware gets us into orbit; governance kee

articles.title: Astrobotic tests advanced rocket engine
articles.description: Astrobotic, a developer of lunar landers and suborbital rockets, has successfully tested an advanced rocket engine that could power those vehicles. The post Astrobotic tests advanced rocket engine appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Ted Cruz pushes back on NASA budget cuts: 'I don't want to wake up one day and look up at the moon and realize the Chinese have beat us there'
articles.description: Sen. Ted Cruz told engineers on NASA's Artemis program, "we'll take care of the politics...so you can do your job," amid steep cuts to NASA's funding in the Presidents fiscal year 2027 budget request.

articles.title: Johnson Leaders Honored by National Space Club & Foundation
articles.description: The National Space Club & Foundation announced its annual award recipients March 13, 2026, in Washington, D.C.   Two dedicated leaders from NASA’s Johnson Space Center were recognized for their contributions to human spaceflight.  Orion Program Manager Howard Hu received

articles.title: NASA Kennedy Prepares Facility for Roman Space Telescope Arrival
articles.description: Preparations are underway for launch of NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope as soon as early September on a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket from Launch Complex 39A at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida. The Roman space telescope will provide deep, panoramic views of the cosmos, generating never-

articles.title: NASA Welcomes Jordan as 63rd Artemis Accords Signatory
articles.description: The Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan signed the Artemis Accords Thursday during a ceremony hosted by NASA at the agency’s headquarters in Washington, becoming the latest nation to commit to responsible space exploration to benefit humanity.  “It is my privile

articles.title: Jordan signs the Artemis Accords
articles.description: Jordan is the latest country to sign the Artemis Accords as NASA works to attract more countries to its lunar exploration efforts. The post Jordan signs the Artemis Accords appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: From 'Starfield' to Artemis: designing moonbases in games and reality
articles.description: Looking at the best games for building lunar settlements, and how they compare to NASA's real-life plans for Artemis Base Camp.

articles.title: NASA still confident that Artemis astronauts will land on the moon in 2028 despite spacesuit delays
articles.description: NASA says it's confident that new spacesuits will be ready for a moon landing in 2028, even though a new report raises the possibility of a multiyear delay.

articles.title: Happy Earth Day! Celebrate with these amazing photos of our planet from space, from Apollo 8 to Artemis 2
articles.description: This Earth Day, we reflect on our home planet and look at Earth from space through history.

articles.title: Meet the US Navy divers who welcomed the Artemis 2 astronauts home from the moon
articles.description: Four Navy divers made a contribution to the historic Artemis 2 moon mission, welcoming its astronauts home to Earth and making sure they were safe and healthy.

articles.title: Celebrate Earth Day with this Artemis 2 shot | Space photo of the day for April 22, 2026
articles.description: Celebrating Earth Day with an image of Earth captured by NASA's Artemis 2 mission around the moon.

articles.title: You've heard of the Space Force. Now there's a 'NASA Force' — but it's not about making space war
articles.description: NASA Force aims to recruit industry talent to the space agency for relatively short stints. Applications are open now in the wake of the Artemis 2 moon mission.

articles.title: NASA rolls out Artemis 3 SLS rocket's huge core stage to gear up for 2027 launch (photo)
articles.description: NASA rolled the Artemis 3 core stage out from the Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans on Monday (April 20). The next stop is the launch site: Kennedy Space Center in Florida.

articles.title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires
articles.description: Following the successful return of Orion, attention for the Artemis program has now shifted to… The post Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires appeared first on NASASpaceFlight.com.
